rationale: Variance expands as n^-2 times a finite sum of fixed-word covariances over length-k word pairs.
Reduced pair classes are aggregated by multiplicity and checked through same/distinct-basepoint labelled constraint exponents.
M30 slopes are compared to n^-1, n^-2, and mixed fits; best least-squares models on the small grid are a*n^-1+b*n^-2.
The argument is for the free two-permutation Schreier benchmark and does not supply a surface-group quotient-family theorem.
All checked k=2,4,6 pair classes have M4-compatible covariance templates of order O(1) before the outer n^-2 normalization.
